title: Delete and Earn Points
description:
You are given an array nums. When you pick a number x you earn x points for each occurrence and then all numbers x-1 and x+1 disappear. Return the maximum points you can earn.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 2*10^4
- 1 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^4
explanation:
Intuition
Group equal numbers; picking value v conflicts only with v-1 and v+1. Reduce to House Robber on a value line where weight[v]=v*count[v].

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Build counts and compute gain for each value
2. Main Algorithm: Run linear robber DP over increasing values considering adjacency by value
3. Return Result: Return best earned

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(U) where U is max value
- Space Complexity: O(U)

Key Insights
- Sorting not required if you use frequency array
- Same transition as house robber

Common Pitfalls
- Make sure to skip values with zero count
- Large U needs array sized by max(nums)

testCases.explanation: When delete_and_earn(nums=[]), the expected result is 0

hints: Zero gains can be skipped implicitly
